root_text.ud7.10:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā kosambiyaṁ viharati ghositārāme.
root_text.ud7.10:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena rañño utenassa uyyānagatassa antepuraṁ daḍḍhaṁ hoti, pañca ca itthisatāni kālaṅkatāni honti sāmāvatīpamukhāni.
root_text.ud7.10:2.1: Atha kho sambahulā bhikkhū pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya kosambiṁ piṇḍāya pāvisiṁsu.
root_text.ud7.10:2.2: Kosambiyaṁ piṇḍāya caritvā pacchābhattaṁ piṇḍapātapaṭikkantā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu.
root_text.ud7.10:2.3: Ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho te bhikkhū bhagavantaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.ud7.10:2.4: “idha, bhante, rañño utenassa uyyānagatassa antepuraṁ daḍḍhaṁ, pañca ca itthisatāni kālaṅkatāni sāmāvatīpamukhāni.
root_text.ud7.10:2.5: Tāsaṁ, bhante, upāsikānaṁ kā gati ko abhisamparāyo”ti?
root_text.ud7.10:3.1: “Santettha, bhikkhave, upāsikāyo sotāpannā, santi sakadāgāminiyo, santi anāgāminiyo.
root_text.ud7.10:3.2: Sabbā tā, bhikkhave, upāsikāyo anipphalā kālaṅkatā”ti.
root_text.ud7.10:4.1: Atha kho bhagavā etamatthaṁ viditvā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:

translation_text.ud7.10:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Kosambī, in Ghosita’s Monastery.
translation_text.ud7.10:1.3: Now at that time, while King Udena was visiting a park, his royal compound burned down. Five hundred women died, with Queen Sāmāvatī at their head.
translation_text.ud7.10:2.1: Then several mendicants robed up in the morning and, taking their bowls and robes, entered Kosambī for alms.
translation_text.ud7.10:2.2: Then, after the meal, when they returned from almsround, they went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and told him had happened. They asked the Buddha,
translation_text.ud7.10:2.5: “Sir, where have those laywomen been reborn in the next life?”
translation_text.ud7.10:3.1: “Among those laywomen there were stream-enterers, once-returners, and non-returners.
translation_text.ud7.10:3.2: None of those laywomen died without some fruit of the practice.”
translation_text.ud7.10:4.1: Then, understanding this matter, on that occasion the Buddha expressed this heartfelt sentiment:
translation_text.ud7.10:5.1: “The world is caught up in delusion,
translation_text.ud7.10:5.2: but is looked on as making sense.
translation_text.ud7.10:5.3: The fool caught up in attachment
translation_text.ud7.10:5.6: but for one who sees, there is nothing.”

variant_text.ud7.10:1.3: utenassa → udenassa (bj, sya-all, pts-vp-pli1) | pañca ca itthisatāni → pañca itthisatāni (bj, sya-all, pts-vp-pli1)
variant_text.ud7.10:5.5: Sassatoriva → sassati viya (bj, pts-vp-pli1); assarī viya (ne4:261 [Vibhaṅga/ Paññattihāravibhaṅga])
